body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: unified ReaderDevice replaces split reading
body.description:
**What changed:** A unified ReaderDevice (URI resolver + blob cache + two output modes) was built as a rack device, consolidating SummarizerDevice and Igor's reading_tool.py/reading_engine.py/book_learner.py.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Igor's reading_tool.py still in place alongside ReaderDevice — risk of running both without a clean cutover, producing duplicate nodes
- Calibre:// handler introduces dependency on Calibre CLI/metadata.db presence — silent failures if Calibre not installed or library moved
- Blob cache accumulates with no eviction policy — disk exhaustion risk on high-volume reading
- Node extraction quality may drift from book_learner.py's current behavior — embeddings could shift for the same source material
- comms:// deferred but URI resolver pattern assumes it fits — may need design rework when added

**Signals to watch:** Igor's reading_tool.py being invoked after ReaderDevice ships (check logs for book_learner_progress/ writes); disk usage in ~/.unseen_university/blobs/; calibre:// error rate in reader logs; node count per source vs prior book_learner baseline.

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-11 or when first ReaderDevice reading run completes.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
